compiled_state.title: As the DSO workflow, I can fetch all unresolved PR comments from a GitHub PR and normalize them to a structured JSON file
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new shell script (pr-comment-response.sh) that fetches all unresolved comments from a GitHub PR — both inline review comments and top-level conversation comments — and writes them to a normalized JSON file for downstream action handlers.
**Why**: All three response handlers (accept, defend, defer) require a consistent, pre-processed view of unresolved PR comments. This script is the data foundation for the entire comment-response pipeline.
**Scope**:
- IN: Paginated fetch of inline review comments (via GitHub pull request review comments API) and top-level conversation comments (via GitHub issues comments API); API layer probe at startup — use GitHub MCP server tools (v0.31.0+) if configured and responsive; fallback to gh api --paginate on any MCP failure (401, 403, tool-not-found, timeout); normalization to JSON schema {comment_id, root_comment_id, thread_url, body, author, is_inline, already_replied, defense_comment_id, path, position} where path is the file being commented on (for inline comments) and position is the line number; root_comment_id resolution by walking in_reply_to_id chain to null (the true thread root) — guard against cycles with max-depth limit; deduplication: threads where any reply begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply --> are excluded from the output (already_replied=true); API probe logic implemented as a shared reusable function usable by the standalone skill; empty result: {"comments": [], "skipped_count": <N>}; non-zero exit with "ERROR: <message>" on API failure; gh CLI minimum version check at startup with clear error if below minimum; rate-limit response detection with backoff on paginated fetch; shared thread-reply posting function (is_inline-aware endpoint routing: inline → POST /pulls/{n}/comments/{root_comment_id}/replies; top-level → POST /issues/{n}/comments) for use by all action handlers
- OUT: Comment classification (handled by standalone skill, Story 5); action handler logic; MCP server configuration or installation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running pr-comment-response.sh against a test PR produces a normalized JSON file matching the schema {comment_id, root_comment_id, thread_url, body, author, is_inline, already_replied, defense_comment_id, path, position}
  ← Satisfies: SC1 schema specification (extended with path/position per adversarial review RT-2)
- When this story is complete, root_comment_id for any reply comment is the true thread root (walked to null), and the walk terminates even for malformed/circular references (max-depth guard)
  ← Satisfies: "root_comment_id is the top-level comment in the thread (resolved by walking in_reply_to_id to the true root for replies)" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, threads that already contain a reply beginning with <!-- dso-agent-reply --> are excluded from output
  ← Satisfies: "comments are excluded when the thread contains a reply beginning with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, the script exits 0 and writes {"comments": [], "skipped_count": N} when no unresolved comments exist
  ← Satisfies: "when no unresolved comments exist, exits 0 and writes..." (SC1)
- When this story is complete, the script exits non-zero with "ERROR: <message>" when the GitHub API call fails
  ← Satisfies: "exits non-zero with ERROR: on API failure" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, the script includes a shared thread-reply posting function with is_inline-aware endpoint routing, used by all three action handlers to avoid duplicated reply logic
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding RT-1 (shared reply posting utility)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Security] GITHUB_TOKEN accessed in this script — ensure it never appears in script output, debug messages, or error traces (A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures)
- [Reliability] root_comment_id chain walk must terminate — implement max-depth guard (e.g., 50 iterations) or visited-set to prevent infinite loop on malformed circular references
- [Reliability] GitHub API rate limit during paginated comment fetch — detect rate-limit responses (HTTP 403/429 with retry-after) and implement backoff/retry
- [Maintainability] MCP probe logic at startup must be implemented as a reusable function (e.g., in plugins/dso/hooks/lib/ or a new shared lib) so it can be called identically by pr-comment-response.sh and the standalone skill without duplication
- [Maintainability] Normalized JSON schema ({comment_id, root_comment_id, thread_url, body, author, is_inline, already_replied, defense_comment_id, path, position}) is the shared contract between this story and Stories 2, 3, 4 — define it once (schema constant or documented interface) and validate against it in tests
- [Integration] gh CLI minimum version check at startup — emit a clear human-readable error if below minimum version required for --paginate

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
